orchestrator_notes: All 3 phases executed in a single session (sufficient context). No gated chain needed.

sessions.title: FastMCP SDK Transport Replacement
planned_tasks: Create mcp_sdk_server.py with all 27 tools registered
tasks_completed: Researched SDK: FastMCP constructor, @mcp.tool() decorator, streamable_http_app(), Context injection, ASGI middleware auth
Upgraded mcp package from 1.15.0 to 1.26.0
Created api/endpoints/mcp_sdk_server.py with all 27 tools via @mcp.tool() decorators
Central _call_tool() dispatch with signature-based tenant_key injection
MCPAuthMiddleware (ASGI) for Bearer token auth (JWT + API key dual-path)
build_mcp_app() factory for Starlette sub-app with middleware
Mounted at /mcp-sdk temporarily, verified 27 tools registered
deviations: 27 tools not 30 — original handover overestimated. tool_map has exactly 27 entries.
Used stateless_http=True + json_response=True for simplicity (passive server, no SSE needed)
fetch_context parameter renamed from 'format' to 'output_format' to avoid Python builtin shadow (matches ToolAccessor internal name)
sessions.notes_for_next: SDK mount pattern: FastMCP(streamable_http_path='/') + app.mount('/mcp', build_mcp_app()). Tenant context flows via scope['state']['tenant_key'] set by MCPAuthMiddleware, read by _resolve_tenant() via ctx.request_context.request.
cascading_impacts: fetch_context parameter 'format' now exposed as 'output_format' to MCP clients
sessions.summary: Created mcp_sdk_server.py (690 lines) with 27 tools registered via FastMCP SDK. All tools delegate to existing ToolAccessor. Auth middleware validates Bearer tokens and injects tenant_key.

sessions.title: Security Re-integration & Old Code Removal
planned_tasks: Wire Bearer token auth middleware into FastMCP pipeline
Remove old mcp_http router from app.py
tasks_completed: Auth middleware already built in 0846a (combined phases)
Promoted SDK endpoint from /mcp-sdk to /mcp in app.py
Removed mcp_http import from app.py endpoint imports
Removed include_router(mcp_http.router) from router registration
Verified app loads cleanly with /mcp Mount route
Verified 15 existing mcp_http tests still pass (file still exists, just not loaded at runtime)
Lint clean: ruff check api/ src/ passes
deviations: Did NOT delete mcp_http.py file — 3 test files still import from it directly. Tests pass as-is. File is dead code at runtime but deleting it would break tests. Left for future cleanup or test rewrite.
SDK schema validation replaces _TOOL_SCHEMA_PARAMS — SDK only passes declared parameters to tool functions. No need for manual allowlist.
sessions.notes_for_next: mcp_http.py still exists on disk (dead at runtime). 3 test files import from it: test_bearer_auth_migration.py, test_0411a_phase_parameter.py, test_mcp_oauth_auth.py. These tests exercise the old endpoint directly — they should be rewritten to test the SDK endpoint, or deleted if coverage is redundant.
cascading_impacts: mcp_http.py is dead code at runtime but still on disk for test compatibility
sessions.summary: Promoted SDK endpoint to /mcp, removed old router from app.py. Auth middleware handles JWT + API key dual-path. Old file kept for test compat.

chain_summary: All 3 phases completed in single session. Custom JSON-RPC transport replaced with official MCP SDK (FastMCP + Streamable HTTP). 27 tools registered, auth middleware wired, old router removed from app.py. 3 docs updated. Old mcp_http.py kept on disk for test compat (dead at runtime).
